episodes.title: How a Male Supermodel Lost Everything To a CULT — Hoyt Richards
episodes.description:
Hoyt Richards, a man who appeared to have everything. During the late 1980s and early 1990s, Richards became one of the highest-paid male models in the world, working for luxury fashion houses including Versace, Valentino, Ralph Lauren, and Burberry. While fashion magazines celebrated his success, almost nobody knew he was secretly funding a group called Eternal Values, led by Manhattan socialite Frederick von Mierers, who claimed to be an alien consciousness from the star Arcturus. Richards would spend years surrendering nearly all of his earnings, eventually losing an estimated $4–5 million while living under strict control.

The story begins on a Nantucket beach in 1978, where a teenage Richards encountered von Mierers and was drawn into a world of astrology, Eastern philosophy, and promises of higher purpose. What followed was a decades-long process of manipulation involving nightly confessions, financial dependence, isolation from family, restrictions on relationships, and psychological control. Even as Richards became one of the most recognizable men in fashion, he slept on floors, obeyed cult rules, and financed a movement that eventually relocated to a secluded compound in North Carolina to prepare for a predicted apocalypse.

After von Mierers died in 1990, the group became even more extreme. Members stockpiled food, gold, and weapons while waiting for the end of the world, and Richards made multiple failed escape attempts before finally breaking free in 1999. With little money left and his modeling career largely behind him, he began the difficult process of rebuilding his life and understanding what had happened to him.

The documentary also examines a troubling question: how could one of the most visible figures in fashion remain trapped in a cult for so long without intervention? Richards' story reveals how intelligence, success, and public admiration offer little protection against coercive influence, and how industries built around image can sometimes overlook profound suffering hidden in plain sight.

The renewed interest in his story follows the June 2026 HBO documentary series Bring Me the Beauties: A Model Cult, which brought the bizarre history of Eternal Values to a new audience.
